title: Orlando
description: Orlando — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: "A knowledge wiki for Virginia Woolf's Orlando: A Biography"

memes.description: "Front matter — Dedication"

memes.label: "Orlando — Table of Contents"
memes.description: 'Table of Contents'

memes.label: A Visit to a Nobleman in the Country
memes.description: Nick Greene's satirical pamphlet ridiculing Orlando, dashed off in a single afternoon for profit and revenge

memes.label: Sir Adrian Scrope
memes.description: British naval commander who delivered Orlando's patent of nobility and the Order of the Bath to the embassy in Constantinople during Orlando's tenure as Ambassador.

memes.label: "Aethelbert: A Tragedy in Five Acts"
memes.description: A fictional early tragedy written by the young Orlando during the Elizabethan period, representing his youthful literary ambitions.

memes.label: "Aethelbert: A Tragedy in Five Acts"
memes.description: Orlando's first literary work — a boyish Elizabethan tragedy abandoned for poetry and the oak tree

memes.label: Alexander Pope
memes.description: The great Augustan poet, who appears in Chapter IV as part of the eighteenth-century literary society Orlando frequents

memes.label: Androgyny
memes.description: The blending or coexistence of masculine and feminine traits within a single person, a concept central to Woolf's exploration of gender identity through Orlando.

memes.label: Archduke Harry / Archduchess Harriet
memes.description: The Archduke who pursues Orlando as a man and later, as the Archduchess Harriet, pursues Orlando as a woman

memes.label: Art and Poetry
memes.description: The lifelong creative vocation — Orlando's poem "The Oak Tree" as the unifying thread across all selves and centuries

memes.label: Art versus Nature
memes.description: The tension and dialogue between artistic creation and the natural world, a dialectic that structures the novel's treatment of the oak tree, the poem, and Orlando's divided loyalties.
aliases: the relationship between art and nature

memes.label: Nicholas Benedict Bartolus
memes.description: The sea captain who escorts Orlando on the voyage from Constantinople back to England in Chapter IV, providing comic commentary on London landmarks

memes.label: Ben Jonson
memes.description: Elizabethan playwright and contemporary of Shakespeare, discussed by Nick Greene in the Cock Tavern scene as part of Orlando's literary education in the Elizabethan literary world.

memes.label: The Biographer
memes.description: Woolf's parodic narrator-biographer who self-consciously performs the act of writing Orlando's life while undermining biographical conventions.

memes.label: Biography
memes.description: The novel's playful critique and reinvention of biographical form as both literary genre and way of knowing a life

memes.label: Blackfriars
memes.description: Orlando's London residence in the Blackfriars district; the site where Sasha and Orlando plan their elopement, and where Orlando returns to face legal challenges after her transformation

memes.label: Bloomsbury Group
memes.description: The circle of writers, artists, and intellectuals centred on the Bloomsbury district of London, of which Virginia Woolf was a central member and whose ethos informs the novel's spirit of intellectual freedom.

memes.label: The Bosphorus
memes.description: The strait dividing Europe and Asia at Constantinople, significant in the novel as a geographic and symbolic boundary.

memes.label: British Embassy at Constantinople
memes.description: The British diplomatic mission in Constantinople where Orlando serves as Ambassador, a pivotal setting in the novel's middle section.

memes.label: Brusa
memes.description: The city of Bursa in Anatolia, near which Orlando lives with the gipsy tribe after departing Constantinople.

memes.label: Canute
memes.description: Orlando's elkhound, whose joyful greeting upon her return from Constantinople and later appearances embody the constancy of animal companionship amid the flux of human identity

memes.label: Cape Horn
memes.description: A recurring geographical motif in Orlando, representing adventure, masculinity, separation, and the bond between Orlando and her husband Shelmerdine, who is perpetually sailing around it.

memes.label: Index of Characters
memes.description: Complete index of all characters appearing in Virginia Woolf's Orlando, sorted alphabetically.

memes.label: Cheapside
memes.description: A commercial street and printing district in Elizabethan London, where Orlando had his first play printed — a spatial marker of the boundary between aristocratic amateurism and professional authorship

memes.label: Christopher Marlowe
memes.description: Elizabethan playwright and poet, recalled in Orlando through Nicholas Greene's vivid memories of the Cock Tavern literary scene, and later through Orlando's own literary imagination

memes.label: Chronological Index
memes.description: A chronological overview of the major periods and events in Orlando, providing a temporal map of the novel's three-century span.

memes.label: Clorinda
memes.description: One of three noblewomen briefly engaged to Orlando in the Elizabethan period; a sweet-mannered lady whose qualities ultimately repel him

memes.label: Cock Tavern
memes.description: An Elizabethan tavern in Fleet Street, London, where Christopher Marlowe, Shakespeare, Ben Jonson, and other literary figures gathered — recalled by Nick Greene in his conversation with Orlando.

memes.label: Cocoa Tree
memes.description: An 18th-century chocolate house in London where Joseph Addison, John Dryden, and Alexander Pope gathered, observed by Orlando upon her return to England from Constantinople.

memes.label: Complete Index
memes.description: A comprehensive A-Z index of all entries in the Orlando wiki — characters, concepts, events, places, and works.

memes.label: Index of Concepts
memes.description: A thematic index of all concepts — ideas, themes, and abstractions — explored in Virginia Woolf's Orlando, sorted alphabetically.

memes.label: Index of Concepts
memes.description: Complete index of all thematic concepts and ideas explored in Virginia Woolf's Orlando.

memes.label: Constantinople
memes.description: The Ottoman capital where Orlando serves as British Ambassador, undergoes his mysterious seven-day sleep, and experiences the gender transformation that divides the novel

memes.label: Orlando's Appointment to Constantinople
memes.description: Orlando's appointment as Ambassador Extraordinary to Constantinople under King Charles II — the diplomatic mission that leads to the Dukedom, the Ramadan entertainment, the seven-day sleep, and the novel's central transformation

memes.label: The Constantinople Insurrection
memes.description: A popular uprising in Constantinople that occurs during Orlando's ambassadorship, coinciding with Orlando's mysterious seven-day trance and gender transformation.

memes.label: Convention and Society
memes.description: The constraints and absurdities of social convention across four centuries of English life

memes.label: The Coronation Carnival
memes.description: The carnival on the frozen Thames during the coronation of James I — the setting where Orlando first encounters Sasha, the Muscovite princess who becomes his first great love

memes.label: Countess of R.
memes.description: An aristocratic lady who pursues Orlando romantically during his time in London society, illustrating the social pressures of gender and marriage in the 18th century.

memes.label: Court of Queen Elizabeth I
memes.description: The royal court of Queen Elizabeth I, where Orlando begins his life as a young nobleman in the opening chapter of the novel.

memes.label: Death and Mortality
memes.description: The presence of death as a recurring shadow across Orlando's extended life and the lives of those around them

memes.label: Disguise and Performance
memes.description: The use of disguise and the performance of social roles, a recurring motif in Orlando's navigation of gender and across historical periods.

memes.label: Don Juan
memes.description: Lord Byron's satirical epic Don Juan, alluded to in the novel's exploration of romantic love and the figure of the libertine.

memes.label: Edmund Spenser
memes.description: The Elizabethan poet, author of The Faerie Queene, referenced in the novel's Elizabethan literary world.

memes.label: Elizabethan Literary Culture
memes.description: The literary world of Elizabethan England as depicted in Woolf's novel — a golden age of poetry and drama refracted through Orlando's youthful ambitions and Nicholas Greene's satirical judgments

memes.label: Embassy Garden at Constantinople
memes.description: The garden of the British Embassy in Constantinople where Orlando retreats during the insurrection and where elements of the transformation unfold.

memes.label: Englishness
memes.description: English national identity as a recurring backdrop and object of ironic reflection throughout Orlando

memes.label: Euphrosyne
memes.description: The Irish noblewoman to whom Orlando was briefly betrothed in the Elizabethan era — the third and most serious of his early flames

memes.label: Index of Events
memes.description: A chronological index of all key events and plot milestones in Virginia Woolf's Orlando, sorted alphabetically.

memes.label: Index of Events
memes.description: Complete index of all key events and plot points in Virginia Woolf's Orlando.

memes.label: Fame and Reputation
memes.description: The pursuit and emptiness of literary fame as Orlando navigates changing literary cultures across centuries

memes.label: Favilla
memes.description: One of three noblewomen briefly engaged to Orlando in the Elizabethan period; a socially ambitious Somersetshire gentleman's daughter who loses Orlando through cruelty to a spaniel

memes.label: Fleet Street
memes.description: Fleet Street, London — the haunt of Elizabethan playwrights in Nick Greene's nostalgic memory, and the noisy urban thoroughfare that represents the literary life Orlando's country estate cannot provide

memes.label: Galata Bridge
memes.description: A bridge spanning the Golden Horn in Constantinople, where Orlando observed the city's daily life during his tenure as British Ambassador and where he wandered incognito at night.

memes.label: Gender and Sexuality
memes.description: The fluidity of gender and the social construction of sexual identity in Orlando

memes.label: Gipsy Camp near Brusa
memes.description: The encampment of a gipsy tribe in the hills near Brusa where Orlando lives after leaving Constantinople following the transformation.

memes.label: Greenwich
memes.description: The royal court at Greenwich, where the new King James I seizes the opportunity of the Great Frost to stage a spectacular carnival on the frozen Thames

memes.label: "Gulliver's Travels"
memes.description: Jonathan Swift's satirical masterpiece — explicitly referenced in Chapter IV as an interpretive key to the Augustan literary spirit, alongside the author's dramatic entrance into Orlando's salon
aliases: Travels into Several Remote Nations of the World

memes.label: "Hamlet"
memes.description: Shakespeare's tragedy — invoked in Chapter II through Nick Greene's gossip about how Shakespeare's plays went to press, and part of the Elizabethan literary pantheon that haunts Orlando's imagination

memes.label: Hampton Court Palace
memes.description: The royal palace on the Thames, associated with the Tudor and Stuart monarchs, referenced in the novel's landscape of English power.

memes.label: History and Historiography
memes.description: The writing of history as both subject and method in Orlando, a novel that functions simultaneously as a work of historical fiction and as a critique of historical writing.

memes.label: Hogarth Press
memes.description: The publishing house founded by Virginia and Leonard Woolf, self-referentially invoked in Orlando as the novel's own publisher — a metafictional gesture that collapses the boundary between fictional biography and material book

memes.label: Hyde Park
memes.description: London's royal park where Orlando sat beneath a tree reflecting on literature, life, and the challenge of finding her own writer's voice.

memes.label: Identity
memes.description: The continuity and multiplicity of personal identity across time, gender, and social role in Orlando

memes.label: Immortality
memes.description: The concept of living beyond a normal human lifespan, central to Orlando's experience as a character who lives over three hundred years without aging in a conventional manner.

memes.label: John Donne
memes.description: The metaphysical poet and Dean of St. Paul's Cathedral — invoked in Orlando as a benchmark of canonical literature against which the novel implicitly measures its own form

memes.label: John Dryden
memes.description: Poet, playwright, and critic (1631–1700) whom Orlando observed at the Cocoa Tree coffeehouse and later encountered in London literary society.

memes.label: Jonathan Swift
memes.description: The great Augustan satirist and author of Gulliver's Travels, encountered in eighteenth-century literary London

memes.label: Joseph Addison
memes.description: The Augustan essayist and co-founder of The Spectator, encountered by Orlando in eighteenth-century London

memes.label: Kew Gardens
memes.description: Kew Gardens, glimpsed from a window in the Victorian era as Orlando contemplates the passage of time, nature, and the persistent desire that propels her narrative forward

memes.label: King Charles II
memes.description: King of England (1660–1685) whose Restoration court Orlando encountered upon returning to England as a woman in the late seventeenth century.

memes.label: King James I
memes.description: King of England (1603–1625) during whose reign Orlando served as ambassador to Constantinople, and the Great Frost occurred.

memes.label: King Lear
memes.description: Shakespearean tragedy referenced in Orlando as an example of hasty Elizabethan playwriting.

memes.label: Lady R.
memes.description: The Countess of R., an influential eighteenth-century salonnière who presided over literary London and hosted Orlando at her celebrated gatherings

memes.label: Levant Company
memes.description: The English trading company that managed commercial relations with the Ottoman Empire, providing the diplomatic infrastructure through which Orlando's ambassadorship was arranged.

memes.label: List of Characters
memes.description: A comprehensive index of all characters appearing in Virginia Woolf's Orlando, organised by period and type.

memes.label: List of Concepts
memes.description: An index of major themes and concepts explored in Virginia Woolf's Orlando.

memes.label: List of Events
memes.description: A chronological index of key events in Virginia Woolf's Orlando.

memes.label: List of Places
memes.description: A comprehensive index of locations and places appearing in Virginia Woolf's Orlando.

memes.label: List of Works
memes.description: An index of literary works referenced, created, or alluded to in Virginia Woolf's Orlando.

memes.label: Literary Betrayal
memes.description: The artist's vulnerability to the critic's power — how literary judgment can become an act of personal betrayal

memes.label: Literary Criticism
memes.description: A recurring theme in Orlando concerning the relationship between creative writing and critical judgement, embodied primarily in the figure of Nick Greene and his evolving role across the centuries.

memes.label: London
memes.description: London as it appears across four centuries in Woolf's Orlando — site of the Frost Fair, literary society, and Orlando's changing urban identity

memes.label: Lord Palmerston
memes.description: The nineteenth-century legal authority who formally pronounced Orlando's sex to be female, settling the lawsuits that followed Orlando's return from Constantinople

memes.label: Love and Desire
memes.description: The transformative, often painful, and gender-transcending arc of romantic love across centuries — from Elizabethan passion through farce to androgynous union

memes.label: Marmaduke Bonthrop Shelmerdine
memes.description: Orlando's husband, a sea captain perpetually sailing round Cape Horn, whose gender ambiguity mirrors Orlando's and creates a marriage of unconventional freedom

memes.label: Marshall & Snelgrove's
memes.description: The London department store where Orlando shops in the Victorian era, representing the material culture of the nineteenth century and the palimpsest of time in modern commerce.

memes.label: Melancholy
memes.description: Orlando's recurring melancholic moods — sudden descents into gloom and contemplation of death — as a counterpoint to the novel's exuberant vitality and a structural device marking transitions between eras.

memes.label: Memory
memes.description: Memory as the medium through which the past remains present and identity coheres across centuries

memes.label: Most Referenced Pages
memes.description: A statistical ranking of the most referenced and cross-linked pages in the Orlando wiki, reflecting centrality and interconnectedness.

memes.label: Mr. Dupper
memes.description: Orlando's chaplain and a longstanding household servant who, with Mrs. Grimsditch, represents the domestic continuity that persists across centuries

memes.label: Mrs. Banting
memes.description: The midwife who delivers Orlando's son in the Victorian era, marking Orlando's transition into motherhood and the domestic sphere.

memes.label: Mrs. Grimsditch
memes.description: Orlando's loyal housekeeper — part of the domestic continuity that endures across centuries of change

memes.label: Mrs. Stewkley
memes.description: A housekeeper or domestic servant at Orlando's estate, representing the continuity of domestic life across the centuries of Orlando's existence.

memes.label: Muscovite Embassy
memes.description: The diplomatic mission from Russia to the English court during the reign of James I, whose arrival during the Great Frost brought the Princess Sasha to London and initiated Orlando's first great love affair.

memes.label: nature
memes.description: Redirect to nature-and-the-oak-tree

memes.label: Nature and the Oak Tree
memes.description: The symbolic centrality of the oak tree and the natural world as anchors of identity, creativity, and continuity in Orlando

memes.label: Nell
memes.description: A London prostitute whom Orlando encounters while cross-dressing in the eighteenth century — a figure from the urban demimonde whose company Orlando finds unexpectedly liberating

memes.label: Nell Gwyn
memes.description: Restoration actress and mistress of Charles II, who appears fleetingly in Orlando's social world upon her return to England, representing the theatrical and libertine character of the Restoration court.

memes.label: Nick Greene
memes.description: The Elizabethan poet and later Victorian critic who represents the literary establishment Orlando both needs and transcends

memes.label: The Oak Tree Estate
memes.description: Orlando's ancestral country estate in Kent — the novel's central spatial anchor, crowned by a solitary oak tree on a high mound

memes.label: Order of the Garter
memes.description: The highest order of chivalry in England, awarded to Orlando during his time at the Elizabethan court.

memes.label: Orlando
memes.description: The protagonist of Virginia Woolf's Orlando — a nobleman and poet who lives for four centuries and changes sex

memes.label: Orlando at the Elizabethan Court
memes.description: Orlando's introduction to the court of Queen Elizabeth I as a handsome youth of sixteen, marking the beginning of his life at the centre of English power.

memes.label: Orlando in the Twentieth Century
memes.description: The novel's final chapter, set in 1928, in which Orlando drives a motor car, shops at a department store, and experiences the simultaneity of modern consciousness.

memes.label: Orlando Meets Alexander Pope
memes.description: Orlando's social encounter with the poet Alexander Pope in 18th-century London, illustrating the literary salon culture of the Augustan age.

memes.label: Orlando Meets Nick Greene
memes.description: Orlando's first encounter with the poet and critic Nick Greene at the Oak Tree estate, establishing a relationship that recurs across the centuries.

memes.label: Orlando's Exile
memes.description: The period of rural seclusion following the disasters of the Great Frost and Thaw, when Orlando retires to his great country house and dedicates himself to writing — a retreat from society that proves foundational to his literary identity

memes.label: Orlando's Lawsuits
memes.description: The three major legal suits brought against Orlando upon her return to England — charging that she was dead, that she was a woman, and that her marriage to Rosina Pepita produced three sons who claimed her inheritance

memes.label: Orlando's Marriage
memes.description: Orlando's sudden betrothal and marriage to Marmaduke Bonthrop Shelmerdine — the event that resolves the novel's central romantic quest, uniting two people who each contain both genders

memes.label: Orlando's Return to England
memes.description: Orlando's journey back to England from Constantinople after the transformation, aboard a British ship, marking the transition to life as a woman in English society.

memes.label: Orlando's Trance
memes.description: Orlando's seven-day trance in Constantinople — the deep sleep during which Orlando changes sex, heralded by a masque of Purity, Chastity, and Modesty, and punctuated by the trumpet blast of Truth

memes.label: Orlando's Transformation
memes.description: The central event of Woolf's Orlando — during a week-long trance in Constantinople, Orlando transforms from man to woman at age thirty

memes.label: Orlando's Youth
memes.description: The period of Orlando's youth as a beautiful boy at the Elizabethan court, when beauty and innocence win the Queen's favour and set the course of a long life.

memes.label: "Othello"
memes.description: Shakespeare's tragedy of jealousy and race — invoked alongside Hamlet and King Lear in Nick Greene's gossip about the material conditions of Elizabethan playwriting

memes.label: Paradise Lost
memes.description: John Milton's epic poem Paradise Lost (1667), referenced in the novel as part of the literary tradition that Orlando inherits and the biographer invokes.

memes.label: Park Lane
memes.description: A major thoroughfare in London's Mayfair district, significant in Orlando as the location of her residence during the Victorian era and the setting for contemplative moments in the novel's modern sections.

memes.label: Patronage
memes.description: The system by which artists and writers were supported by wealthy patrons, a recurring motif as Orlando moves through different literary periods.

memes.label: Index of Persons
memes.description: A comprehensive index of all persons (characters and historical figures) appearing in Virginia Woolf's Orlando, sorted alphabetically.

memes.label: Index of Places
memes.description: A geographical index of all places — real, fictional, and hybrid — that appear in Virginia Woolf's Orlando, sorted alphabetically.

memes.label: Index of Places
memes.description: Complete index of all locations and places appearing in Virginia Woolf's Orlando.

memes.label: Publication of The Oak Tree
memes.description: The eventual publication and prize-winning of Orlando's long poem "The Oak Tree" in the Victorian era, marking the culmination of centuries of work.
aliases: The Oak Tree wins the prize

memes.label: Queen Elizabeth I
memes.description: The ageing Queen Elizabeth I, who favours the young Orlando and sets the Elizabethan court scenes in motion

memes.label: Queen Victoria
memes.description: Queen of the United Kingdom (1837–1901), whose reign defines the Victorian era that Orlando experiences in the mid-nineteenth century — a period of enforced respectability, heavy draperies, and stifling domesticity.

memes.label: Reading and Interpretation
memes.description: The act of reading as a creative and transformative process, a theme implicit in the novel's engagement with literary tradition and explicit in the biographer's interpretive struggles.

memes.label: Richmond
memes.description: Richmond — a royal residence where Queen Elizabeth plots Orlando's ambitious career, and later a destination for romantic drives and marriage proposals in the eighteenth century

memes.label: Rosina Pepita
memes.description: A dancer of Romani origin whom Orlando (as a man) marries in Constantinople, whose existence later becomes grounds for complex legal challenges to Orlando's identity and inheritance

memes.label: Russian Embassy
memes.description: The Russian diplomatic mission to London during the reign of James I, bringing the entourage that includes the princess Sasha.

memes.label: Rustum
memes.description: Orlando's guide and companion who brings her from Constantinople to the gipsy camp — the figure whose scepticism about English values lingers in Orlando's mind across centuries

memes.label: Sasha
memes.description: The Russian princess who becomes Orlando's first great love during the Great Frost, only to abandon him when the thaw comes
aliases: Princess Marousha Stanilovska Dagmar Natasha Iliana Romanovitch

memes.label: Sasha Abandons Orlando
memes.description: The devastating moment when Sasha, the Russian princess, abandons Orlando at the end of the Great Frost, a formative trauma of romantic betrayal.

memes.label: The Serpentine
memes.description: The artificial lake in Hyde Park where Orlando experiences two key moments of psychological revelation — once as a man in the Elizabethan era, and once as a woman in 1928

memes.label: Shakespeare's Sonnets
memes.description: The sonnet sequence by William Shakespeare, alluded to in the novel as part of the Elizabethan literary world Orlando inhabits.

memes.label: Sir Thomas Browne
memes.description: Seventeenth-century physician and prose writer whose works Orlando reads during a period of solitary contemplation, and whom Woolf names among her literary predecessors

memes.label: Solitude
memes.description: Solitude as the necessary condition for creative work and self-knowledge in Orlando's life

memes.label: St. Paul's Cathedral
memes.description: The great cathedral of London, rebuilt by Sir Christopher Wren after the Great Fire, appearing as a landmark in the novel's London scenes.

memes.label: Temple Bar
memes.description: Historic gateway marking the boundary between the City of London and Westminster, where the heads of rebels were displayed on pikes.

memes.label: The Thames
memes.description: The River Thames as the setting for the Great Frost Fair in Chapter I — a frozen carnival where Orlando first encounters Sasha, and the novel's most spectacular transformation of the ordinary into the extraordinary

memes.label: The Admiralty
memes.description: The British government department responsible for the Royal Navy, relevant to Shelmerdine's maritime career and the novel's nautical themes.

memes.label: The Body
memes.description: The physical body as the site of gender, identity, and transformation, a central concern of Orlando's narrative of bodily metamorphosis.

memes.label: The Captain
memes.description: The captain of the vessel Enamoured Lady, who employs Marmaduke Bonthrop Shelmerdine as a sailor on voyages around Cape Horn.

memes.label: The Death of Hercules
memes.description: Orlando's youthful verse tragedy, pressed upon Nick Greene for literary opinion and subsequently mocked in Greene's satire

memes.label: The Deckhand
memes.description: A nameless sailor aboard the Enamoured Lady, representing the ordinary maritime labourers who populate Shelmerdine's world.

memes.label: The Doctor
memes.description: A physician called to attend Orlando during the seven-day trance in Constantinople, whose diagnoses reflect the medical understanding of gender in the 18th century.

memes.label: The Earl of Cumberland
memes.description: An Elizabethan nobleman and merchant adventurer who discovered Orlando and Sukey asleep in a treasure ship, an encounter that prompted the founding of almshouses in the Sheen Road

memes.label: The Enamoured Lady
memes.description: The merchant vessel on which Marmaduke Bonthrop Shelmerdine serves as a sailor, voyaging around Cape Horn, representing the masculine world of maritime adventure in the novel's Victorian sections.

memes.label: The Faerie Queene
memes.description: Edmund Spenser's epic poem The Faerie Queene (1590–1596), a work of Elizabethan literature alluded to in the novel's treatment of chivalry and courtly love.

memes.label: The Frost Fair
memes.description: The carnival city erected on the frozen Thames during the Great Frost, where Orlando first encounters Sasha and falls in love.

memes.label: The Gipsy
memes.description: A gipsy who encounters Orlando after the transformation, offering a perspective on nature and civilization that contrasts sharply with Orlando's English sensibilities.

memes.label: The Great Fire of London
memes.description: The catastrophic fire of 1666 that destroyed much of London during Orlando's absence abroad, reshaping the city to which she returned after her transformation.

memes.label: The Great Frost
memes.description: The catastrophic winter of 1608 — the frozen Thames carnival where Orlando meets Sasha, the defining love and betrayal of his life

memes.label: The Great Plague
memes.description: The Great Plague of 1665, which occurred during Orlando's absence from England — an invisible historical force whose devastation is measurable only in the transformed city Orlando confronts upon her return

memes.label: The Great Thaw
memes.description: The catastrophic thaw that ends the Great Frost in Chapter I — the ice breaks, the carnival city collapses into the Thames, Sasha sails away, and Orlando's first great love is lost in the flood

memes.label: The Gypsy Encounter
memes.description: Orlando's sojourn with a gypsy tribe in the mountains near Broussa after the transformation — a cross-cultural encounter that dramatizes the conflict between civilization and nature, beauty and utility

memes.label: The Levantine
memes.description: A merchant in Constantinople with whom Orlando interacts during the ambassadorship, representing the commercial and cultural crossroads of the Ottoman capital.

memes.label: The London Ball
memes.description: A society ball in London where Orlando, now living as a woman, experiences the rituals of courtship from a female perspective.

memes.label: The Oak Tree
memes.description: The central natural symbol in *Orlando*, representing permanence, rootedness, artistic aspiration, and connection to the English land across centuries of change.

memes.label: The Oak Tree (Manuscript)
memes.description: The physical manuscript of Orlando's long poem "The Oak Tree," which Orlando carries and works on across centuries, serving as a material symbol of continuity and artistic devotion.
aliases: the manuscript of The Oak Tree

memes.label: The Oak Tree (Poem)
memes.description: The poem Orlando writes and rewrites across four centuries — the novel's central symbol of artistic perseverance and the secret thread connecting all her selves

memes.label: The Priest
memes.description: A clergyman present during the events surrounding Orlando's transformation in Constantinople.

memes.label: "The Rape of the Lock"
memes.description: Alexander Pope's mock-epic poem — explicitly invoked in Chapter IV of Orlando as an interpretive key to Pope's character and the Augustan literary spirit

memes.label: The Self and the World
memes.description: The dialectic between Orlando's rich inner life and the external demands of society, history, and embodiment

memes.label: "The Spectator"
memes.description: Joseph Addison and Richard Steele's daily periodical — explicitly referenced and quoted in Chapter IV as a key to understanding 18th-century literary society

memes.label: The Spectator Essays
memes.description: The periodical essays of Joseph Addison and Richard Steele, referenced in the novel as an example of 18th-century literary culture.

memes.label: The Spirit of the Age
memes.description: Woolf's personification of the dominant social and cultural force that shapes each era, particularly manifested in the Victorian compulsion toward gender conformity and marriage.

memes.label: The Strand
memes.description: The Strand, London's great thoroughfare connecting Westminster to the City — a place where poets hawk broadsheets, coaches carry aristocrats through the night, and the city's social hierarchies are inscribed into its geography

memes.label: The Wild Goose
memes.description: The central symbolic motif of Orlando — the wild goose represents the elusive object of poetic and artistic pursuit, a figure for the artistic vocation itself

memes.label: Time
memes.description: The elastic, subjective experience of time across four centuries of narrative consciousness — the novel's central structural principle and its most sustained philosophical investigation

memes.label: Tower of London
memes.description: The historic castle on the Thames, a landmark of English power and imprisonment referenced in the novel's London geography.

memes.label: Transformation
memes.description: Physical and psychological metamorphosis as the central narrative event and governing metaphor of Orlando

memes.label: Truth and Fiction
memes.description: The porous boundary between factual truth and imaginative truth in biography, memory, and self-understanding

memes.label: Victorian Literature
memes.description: Woolf's satirical treatment of Victorian literary culture in *Orlando*, including its prolific output, moral seriousness, and the overwhelming material presence of books in the Victorian home.

memes.label: Wapping
memes.description: The riverside district of East London where Orlando goes to seek low company, where he witnesses the thaw of the Great Frost, and where Captain Bartolus guides the returning Orlando past memories of her former life

memes.label: Westminster Abbey
memes.description: The great abbey church of London, site of coronations and burials, appearing as a landmark of English history and ceremony.

memes.label: Whitehall
memes.description: The royal palace at Whitehall, London — the seat of the monarch where Orlando is summoned to attend Queen Elizabeth and later encounters King Charles II

memes.label: William Shakespeare
memes.description: The greatest English playwright, referenced throughout Orlando as the unattainable ideal of literary achievement

memes.label: Index of Works
memes.description: Complete index of all literary works referenced or created within Virginia Woolf's Orlando.

memes.label: Writing and Authorship
memes.description: The novel's sustained meditation on the act of writing — its rigours, consolations, and relationship to immortality — centred on Orlando's lifelong poem "The Oak Tree."

memes.label: "Xenophila: A Tragedy"
memes.description: One of Orlando's forty-seven juvenile works — a thick Elizabethan tragedy abandoned and later burned
